title: Aregelia compacta
taxonomy: Aregelia compacta is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. It belongs to the genus Aregelia, which is a group of plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Aregelia compacta is further supported by its characteristic features, such as its compact growth habit and showy white flowers. The plant's taxonomy is closely related to that of other Apocynaceae species, which are known for their medicinal and ornamental properties. By studying its classification, botanists can better understand the evolutionary relationships between Aregelia compacta and other plants in the Apocynaceae family.
